content.content:
content: "# Web Document

## Metadata

- title: Renew Your Passport Online
-\
  \ url: https://travel.state.gov/en/passports/renew-replace/online.html
- source:\
  \ U.S. Department of State - Bureau of Consular Affairs
- domain: travel.state.gov
\
  
## Content

Eligible U.S. citizens who want routine service can renew their\
  \ passports online.

Apply now at opr.travel.state.gov.

## Avoid unofficial\
  \ online renewal sites

The only official, authorized place to renew your U.S.\
  \ passport online is opr.travel.state.gov. Other websites or companies claiming\
  \ you can renew your passport online may be fraudulent. No one else can sign and\
  \ submit your online passport application for you. These websites and companies\
  \ charge extra fees and put your personal data at risk. Even if a company has “\
  Gov” in its name, it is not authorized to accept or process online renewal\
  \ applications.

Always check you are on a website ending in .gov, not .com, .us,\
  \ or .org.

The Department of State cannot get involved in any dispute with these\
  \ companies or refund an unauthorized application. You can report an incident to\
  \ the Federal Trade Commission or your state consumer protection office. If your\
  \ personal information has been compromised, visit IdentityTheft.gov for help and\
  \ resources. Check the Better Business Bureau for tips on passport scams.

Visit\
  \ the Courier and Expeditor Companies page for more information.

## Steps to\
  \ renew online

### 1. Confirm you meet the requirements

You can renew online\
  \ if you meet all these requirements:

- The passport you are renewing is or was\
  \ valid for 10 years. It must be expiring within 1 year or have expired less than\
  \ 5 years ago. Check your passport's date of issue and expiration.
- You are age\
  \ 25 or older.
- You are not changing your personal information such as your name\
  \ or sex.
- You are not traveling for at least 6 weeks from the date you will submit\
  \ your application. Online renewals cannot be expedited.
- You are located in a\
  \ U.S. state or territory at the time you submit your application.
- You have your\
  \ passport with you, it is not damaged or mutilated, and you have not reported it\
  \ as lost or stolen.

Do not use your most recent passport after you renew it.\
  \ The Department of State will cancel it so you cannot use it for international\
  \ travel.

If you do not qualify to renew online, you may be able to renew by\
  \ mail or in person at a passport agency or center.

### 2. Get what you need\
  \ before you start

You will need the passport or passports you are renewing,\
  \ a credit or debit card for passport fees, a digital passport photo, and important\
  \ personal details such as your Social Security number and emergency contact. Passport\
  \ fees depend on whether you request a passport book, card, or both. Optional 1\
  \ to 3-day delivery is available for an additional fee.

Important reminders:
\
  
- The Department of State will cancel, or invalidate, the passport you are renewing\
  \ after you submit your application. You cannot use it for international travel\
  \ after it is canceled.
- Do not mail the passport you are renewing. Keep it. Previous\
  \ passports can always be used as proof of citizenship, even when expired.

###\
  \ 3. Complete your application

Once you have gathered your documents and information,\
  \ you are ready to start your application at opr.travel.state.gov. Make sure you\
  \ have time to complete the application. If you come back later, your session may\
  \ expire and you may have to start over.

You must complete your own online application.\
  \ You cannot use a third-party service or have someone submit the application on\
  \ your behalf. If you have technical issues completing your application, dial the\
  \ National Passport Information Center at 877-487-2778.

## After you apply

\
  The Department of State will email status updates after you apply. The updates will\
  \ let you know when your application is in process or approved, and when the passport\
  \ is sent. The Department may also notify you if it needs more information to process\
  \ your application.

If you receive a request for more information, follow the\
  \ instructions in the letter or email. You must respond within 90 days of the date\
  \ on the letter or email.

Contact the Department if you have an issue completing\
  \ your application, if your international travel plans change and you want to request\
  \ expedited service or 1 to 3-day delivery of your completed passport book, or if\
  \ you change your mailing address to another one in the United States.

Last Updated:\
  \ June 29, 2026
"
metadata: {}
title: Renew Your Passport Online
url: https://travel.state.gov/en/passports/renew-replace/online.html

content: "# Web Document\n\n## Metadata\n\n- title: Using a Passport Courier Company\n\
  - url: https://travel.state.gov/en/passports/apply/get-fast/courier-company.html\n\
  - source: U.S. Department of State - Bureau of Consular Affairs\n- domain: travel.state.gov\n\
  \n## Content\n\nCourier companies, or \u201Cpassport expeditors,\u201D are private\
  \ firms. They charge extra fees and some are registered to submit applications and\
  \ pick up passports for customers.\n\nCourier companies can help you prepare your\
  \ application, but they will not get your passport processed faster than you can\
  \ on your own. Save money and avoid potential scams by renewing online before you\
  \ make travel plans, or apply at a passport agency if you have urgent travel.\n\n\
  ## What you need to know before using a third party\n\n### Do research and avoid\
  \ scams\n\nCourier and expeditor companies do not operate as a part of the U.S.\
  \ Department of State. Only some companies are registered with the Department to\
  \ submit applications and pick up passports on behalf of applicants at passport\
  \ agencies.\n\nSome companies may appear to be affiliated with the U.S. government\
  \ because they use logos that look like official government seals. Check the list\
  \ of courier companies to see if a company is registered with the Department of\
  \ State.\n\nAlways research companies first. You will be sharing your most sensitive\
  \ information and documents with them. Read the fine print on each website. The\
  \ Better Business Bureau or a local chamber of commerce may help you decide about\
  \ a company.\n\nThe only place to renew your passport online is opr.travel.state.gov.\
  \ Any other website that claims to offer online passport renewal should be considered\
  \ a scam.\n\n### Know where to apply and be prepared to pay extra fees\n\nUsing\
  \ a courier company will not get you your passport faster than applying at a passport\
  \ agency. The most common times customers use a courier company is if they have\
  \ urgent travel and cannot get to a passport agency.\n\nYou cannot submit an application\
  \ online through a courier company. All passport applications submitted by a courier\
  \ company must be printed out and signed in ink. You must also provide original,\
  \ physical documents when submitting your application through a courier company.\n\
  \nCourier companies charge extra fees for their services. The Department of State\
  \ does not charge a fee to fill out a passport form or to make an appointment at\
  \ a passport agency or acceptance facility. The Department of State does not refund\
  \ fees paid to courier companies. The Department may still need fees from you to\
  \ process or expedite your application.\n\nA registered courier company may hand-carry\
  \ an application or pick up a passport for a customer, but it is not the Department\
  \ of State. A private company cannot replace the official passport application process,\
  \ cannot become the official passport agency, and cannot make an online renewal\
  \ application for you.\n\nBefore using a private courier, review the official options\
  \ for online renewal, applying at a passport agency, or requesting faster service.\
  \ If you have urgent travel, review the passport agency instructions instead of\
  \ assuming a courier company is the official destination.\n"
metadata: {}
title: Using a Passport Courier Company
url: https://travel.state.gov/en/passports/apply/get-fast/courier-company.html

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Change or Correct a Passport

  - url: https://travel.state.gov/en/passports/renew-replace/change-correct-passport.html

  - source: U.S. Department of State - Bureau of Consular Affairs

  - domain: travel.state.gov


  ## Content


  This page explains how to correct or update a U.S. passport due to a name change,
  data error, or printing error. The steps depend on what needs to be changed and
  when the passport was issued or the name was legally changed.


  ## Change your name


  If your name changed less than one year after your passport was issued, you may
  be able to update your passport by mail using Form DS-5504. You must submit the
  form, your current passport, an original or certified name change document, and
  one color passport photo. You will not have to submit any fees unless you request
  optional 1 to 3-day delivery.


  If it has been more than one year since either your passport was issued or your
  name was legally changed, you may be eligible to renew your passport by mail. If
  you are not eligible to renew by mail, you can apply in person. To renew by mail,
  you must provide a certified copy of your legal name change document.


  If you are already using a different name but do not have a legal document to show
  the change, apply in person and submit Form DS-11, evidence of U.S. citizenship,
  valid ID issued in the name you are currently using and a photocopy of that ID,
  one color passport photo, and fees. You may also need Form DS-60 if your name is
  different from what appears on your citizenship evidence and you cannot show the
  new name through marriage or a court order.


  ## Correct a data or printing error


  You can correct your passport if there is a data or printing error. A data error
  may include an issue with your name, sex, or place of birth. A printing error may
  include discoloration, crooked printing, or missing data on the biographical page.
  The Department of State will correct the error at no charge if the passport is still
  valid.


  To correct a data or printing error, submit the following by mail:


  - Form DS-5504, with mailing instructions on the form.

  - Your current passport.

  - One color passport photo.

  - Evidence of the error, such as a U.S. birth certificate showing the correct spelling
  of a last name.


  You will not have to submit any fees for a data or printing error correction.


  If you are reporting the error within one year of the Department of State issuing
  the incorrect passport, the new passport will be valid for 10 years for adults and
  5 years for children under age 16. If you are reporting the error after one year,
  the new passport will be valid until the expiration date of your original, incorrect
  passport.


  ## Change your address


  If you already have a passport and have moved to a new address, you do not need
  to update your current passport with your new address. If you have an application
  in process and have moved to a new address, dial 877-487-2778 or 888-874-7793 for
  TDD/TTY services to update the mailing address on your passport application.


  ## Change your passport photo


  If your photo has a printing error, you can use Form DS-5504 to reapply at no cost.
  If your photo is printed correctly and you need to update your photo, you may use
  Form DS-82 if eligible and must pay the normal fees.


  The correction path is different from routine renewal when the issue is a data or
  printing error. The applicant should use the process that matches the reason for
  the change or correction.

  '
metadata: {}
title: Change or Correct a Passport
url: https://travel.state.gov/en/passports/renew-replace/change-correct-passport.html

content: "# Web Document\n\n## Metadata\n\n- title: Make an Appointment at a Passport\
  \ Agency\n- url: https://travel.state.gov/en/passports/apply/get-fast/make-appointment.html\n\
  - source: U.S. Department of State - Bureau of Consular Affairs\n- domain: travel.state.gov\n\
  \n## Content\n\nYou can make an appointment at a passport agency or center for urgent\
  \ travel when you qualify under the Department of State passport service rules.\
  \ You must have an appointment to go to a passport agency or center.\n\n## How to\
  \ make an appointment if you have not applied\n\nUse the Online Passport Appointment\
  \ System.\n\n1. Go to the Online Passport Appointment System.\n2. Enter information\
  \ about your travel plans to see if you qualify for an appointment. You can make\
  \ an appointment for up to 7 people in your household.\n3. Enter your email address.\
  \ The system will email you a code. Enter that code to keep going. Check your junk\
  \ or spam folder if you do not find the code in your inbox.\n4. Enter your mobile\
  \ phone number. The system will send another code in a text message. Enter that\
  \ code to keep going.\n5. Finish scheduling by choosing your appointment date and\
  \ time.\n6. The Department of State will send an email confirming the details of\
  \ your appointment. The system will hold your appointment for 15 minutes. If you\
  \ do not confirm your appointment within 15 minutes, you will need to start over.\n\
  7. If you need to cancel or change the appointment, select the link in the confirmation\
  \ email.\n\n## If you already applied for your passport\n\nIf you already applied\
  \ for your passport, you need to call the Department of State. If there is time\
  \ to issue your passport before your travel, the Department will notify the agency\
  \ working on your application. If it is not possible to issue your passport before\
  \ your travel, the Department will make an appointment for you.\n\nThe first two\
  \ digits of your 9-digit passport application locator number tell you which agency\
  \ or center is reviewing your application. If you already applied for a passport\
  \ and your plans became more urgent, you can reference this location when you call.\
  \ You can find your application locator number by checking your application status.\n\
  \nFollow these steps:\n\n1. Dial 877-487-2778 to make an appointment. You can call\
  \ Monday to Friday from 8:00 a.m. to 10:00 p.m. Eastern Time, or Saturday and Sunday\
  \ from 10:00 a.m. to 3:00 p.m. Eastern Time. Se habla espa\xF1ol.\n2. If you are\
  \ deaf or hard of hearing, dial 888-874-7793 for TDD/TTY services.\n3. If you qualify\
  \ for an appointment, the Department will provide unique details. These will be\
  \ checked on your appointment day to confirm the appointment is real. If you are\
  \ not the appointment holder, the passport agency or center will not be able to\
  \ see you.\n4. If you need to cancel or change your appointment, call the Department\
  \ of State. The appointment will be given to someone else who needs it. You cannot\
  \ transfer your appointment to another person.\n\n## Find passport agency locations\n\
  \nThe passport agency page lists passport agencies and centers by city and state,\
  \ including Arkansas Passport Center, Atlanta Passport Agency, Boston Passport Agency,\
  \ Buffalo Passport Agency, Charleston Passport Center, Chicago Passport Agency,\
  \ Colorado Passport Agency, Connecticut Passport Agency, Dallas Passport Agency,\
  \ Detroit Passport Agency, and other locations.\n\nThis page explains appointment\
  \ mechanics and contact steps. It does not replace the requirement to qualify for\
  \ urgent service under the passport service timing rules.\n"

content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Get Your Passport Fast

  - url: https://travel.state.gov/en/passports/apply/get-fast.html

  - source: U.S. Department of State - Bureau of Consular Affairs

  - domain: travel.state.gov


  ## Content


  Choose from four passport service options. Pick one based on your travel plans and
  how fast you need your passport.


  Consider the total time it will take to get a passport when booking travel. Mailing
  times can take up to 2 weeks. Consider faster delivery and return shipping when
  you submit your application.


  ## Life-or-death and urgent travel


  If you qualify for one of these options, you need to make an appointment at a passport
  agency or center.


  ### Life-or-death: travel in less than 14 days


  You may qualify for an emergency appointment if you need to travel abroad in the
  next 14 days because an immediate family member outside of the United States has
  died, is dying or in hospice care, or has a life-threatening illness or injury.


  ### Urgent: travel in less than 2-3 weeks


  If you are traveling in less than 2-3 weeks, make an appointment at a passport agency
  or center when you are within 14 calendar days of your international travel date,
  or 28 days if you need a foreign visa.


  ## Expedited: travel in less than 6 weeks


  Choose expedited service if you are traveling in less than 6 weeks from the date
  you submit your application. Expedited service takes 2-3 weeks and does not include
  mailing times. Mailing times may add 2 weeks to the total time to get your passport.


  You can expedite at an acceptance facility or expedite a renewal by mail. You will
  need to pay a $60 expedite fee in addition to the standard application fee.


  ## Routine: travel in 6 weeks or more


  Choose routine service if you are traveling over 6 weeks from the date you submit
  your application. Routine service takes 4-6 weeks and does not include mailing times.
  Mailing times may add 2 weeks to the total time to get your passport.


  You can apply for routine service at an acceptance facility if you are applying
  for the first time or for a child under 16. If you are eligible to renew, you can
  renew online or by mail.


  ## Faster shipping options


  No matter which service option you choose, you can shorten the total time it takes
  to receive your passport by paying for faster shipping.


  When sending your application, you may pay at the acceptance facility for faster
  USPS Priority Mail Express shipping. The cost varies by location.


  For return delivery, you may pay for 1 to 3-day return delivery. Add this cost to
  your application fee and send it to the Department of State as a separate check
  or money order. Do not submit a pre-paid return envelope.


  ## Key points


  Routine passport processing takes 4-6 weeks, plus mailing time. If you need your
  passport sooner, you can choose a faster service.


  Mail can add up to 2 weeks to the total time it takes to receive your passport,
  but you can pay for quicker shipping if needed.


  You must make an appointment for urgent and emergency services. Appointment slots
  may not be available right away.


  Related information includes processing times, use of courier companies, and renewal
  by mail.

  '

content.content:
Your passport appears to have a **valid, recently issued passport with a Department of State data or printing error**, and your international trip is in **11 days**. The appropriate next step is **not routine renewal** and not a private courier.

### Recommended next step: seek urgent passport-agency help

Because your travel is within 14 days:

1. **Try to make an appointment at a U.S. passport agency or center** through the official appointment system:  
   https://travel.state.gov/en/passports/apply/get-fast/make-appointment.html
2. If you cannot schedule online, call the National Passport Information Center at **877-487-2778**.  
   Hours listed by the State Department: Monday–Friday, 8 a.m.–10 p.m. ET; weekends, 10 a.m.–3 p.m. ET.
3. Explain that your **valid passport contains a Department-caused data or printing error** and that you travel internationally on **July 22, 2026**.
4. Bring or prepare:
   - Your current passport
   - **Form DS-5504**
   - One color passport photo
   - Evidence showing the correct information, if it is a data error
   - Proof of international travel
   - Your appointment confirmation

The State Department says valid data or printing errors are generally corrected **without a fee** using DS-5504. However, ordinary correction-by-mail processing may not finish before an 11-day trip, so contact the passport agency promptly.

Use only official **.gov** sources. A courier or expeditor is a private company and does not replace the official passport-agency process or make the application faster.

final_answer:
Your passport appears to have a **valid, recently issued passport with a Department of State data or printing error**, and your international trip is in **11 days**. The appropriate next step is **not routine renewal** and not a private courier.

### Recommended next step: seek urgent passport-agency help

Because your travel is within 14 days:

1. **Try to make an appointment at a U.S. passport agency or center** through the official appointment system:  
   https://travel.state.gov/en/passports/apply/get-fast/make-appointment.html
2. If you cannot schedule online, call the National Passport Information Center at **877-487-2778**.  
   Hours listed by the State Department: Monday–Friday, 8 a.m.–10 p.m. ET; weekends, 10 a.m.–3 p.m. ET.
3. Explain that your **valid passport contains a Department-caused data or printing error** and that you travel internationally on **July 22, 2026**.
4. Bring or prepare:
   - Your current passport
   - **Form DS-5504**
   - One color passport photo
   - Evidence showing the correct information, if it is a data error
   - Proof of international travel
   - Your appointment confirmation

The State Department says valid data or printing errors are generally corrected **without a fee** using DS-5504. However, ordinary correction-by-mail processing may not finish before an 11-day trip, so contact the passport agency promptly.

Use only official **.gov** sources. A courier or expeditor is a private company and does not replace the official passport-agency process or make the application faster.
